# Test the append command: leader replicates log to followers.
# Witness engagement is a separate explicit step.

# Set up two voters; v2 has a longer log.
add-voters
v1: [a1]
v2: [a1 b2]
----
v1: [a1] term=a cfg=(v1 v2 w)
v2: [a1 b2] term=b cfg=(v1 v2 w)

# v2 wins the election, gets a noop entry at term c.
campaign v2
----
v2 campaigns at term c
v2: yes (self)
v1: yes
w:  yes
outcome: won

# v2 appends to v1. v1's log is a prefix of v2's, so it succeeds.
append v2
----
v2 appends through c3
v1: ok
v1: [a1 b2 c3] term=c cfg=(v1 v2 w)
v2: [a1 b2 c3] term=c cfg=(v1 v2 w) leader

# v2 appends with v1's response dropped.
append v2 drop=(v1)
----
v2 appends through c3
v1: ok (dropped)
v1: [a1 b2 c3] term=c cfg=(v1 v2 w)
v2: [a1 b2 c3] term=c cfg=(v1 v2 w) leader

# v2 engages the witness (derives term and lm from v2's state).
engage v2
----
ok
vote:  (c, v2)
acked: (c3, engaged)

# v2 appends again, this time v1 is dropped. The witness is already
# engaged, so the leader can count on it for quorum.
append v2 drop=(v1)
----
v2 appends through c3
v1: ok (dropped)
v1: [a1 b2 c3] term=c cfg=(v1 v2 w)
v2: [a1 b2 c3] term=c cfg=(v1 v2 w) leader

# Release the witness (derives term and lm from v2's state).
release v2
----
ok
vote:  (c, v2)
acked: (c3, released)

# New election: v1 campaigns and wins (its log is now up to date).
campaign v1
----
v1 campaigns at term d
v1: yes (self)
v2: yes
w:  yes
outcome: won

# v1 appends to v2, then engages the witness.
append v1
----
v1 appends through d4
v2: ok
v1: [a1 b2 c3 d4] term=d cfg=(v1 v2 w) leader
v2: [a1 b2 c3 d4] term=d cfg=(v1 v2 w)

engage v1
----
ok
vote:  (d, v1)
acked: (d4, engaged)
